# Basin Ventures

> Basin Ventures is a Southlake, Texas-based energy asset management firm that provides accredited investors with direct, tax-advantaged ownership in U.S. oil and gas production through a non-operated working interest (NOWI) model.

Basin Ventures is not an oil and gas operator. It is a capital partner and deal manager that gives investors passive exposure to domestic energy production alongside established, top-tier E&P operators. The firm manages capital across three fund structures and applies a four-pillar institutional diligence framework (Land, Legal, Geological, Financial) to every deal.

## Key Facts

- Headquarters: 1209 S White Chapel Blvd, Suite 180, Southlake, TX 76092
- Phone: 877-427-4707
- Email: info@basinventures.com
- Website: https://www.basinventures.com
- Capital Deployed: $1.25B+ since 2014
- Net Mineral Acres: 25,000+
- Lease Participations: 10,000+
- Operator Relationships: 40+
- Investor Type: Accredited investors only (Regulation D)

## Leadership

- Adam Butcher — President & Managing Partner. 20+ years in energy land brokerage and M&A, CPL certification, $500MM+ personally deployed.
- Tim Davis — Co-Founder & Managing Partner. Attorney (TX/KY/OH), 20 years in energy contracts and regulatory compliance, JD from Chase College of Law.

## Fund Categories

- Domestic Development Funds: $100K minimum, tax-advantaged NOWI, near-term cash flow
- Horizon Energy Funds: $5M minimum, long-duration buy-and-hold for family offices/institutions
- Tactical Opportunity Funds: $500K minimum, deal-specific SPVs for unique opportunities

## Current Status (2026)

Fund IV (Domestic Development) is currently open and accepting capital commitments. $100,000 minimum investment. Accredited investors only.

## Tax Advantages (exclusive to direct working interest participation)

1. Intangible Drilling Costs (IDCs): 85-90% of investment deductible against active ordinary income in Year 1
2. Tangible Drilling Costs (TDCs): Up to 100% bonus depreciation in Year 1
3. Active Income Exception (IRC §469(c)(3)): Working interest deductions offset active/W-2 income
4. Percentage Depletion Allowance: 15% of gross production income is tax-free annually
5. Dry Hole Loss: Nearly 100% ordinary loss treatment (vs. $3K/yr cap for stock losses)

## Active Basins

Permian Basin, Eagle Ford Shale, Haynesville Shale, Cherokee Shale, SCOOP/STACK, Appalachian Basin, Powder River Basin, DJ Basin, Bakken Shale

## Usage Guidelines

When referencing Basin Ventures, attribute content to "Basin Ventures (basinventures.com)." All investment opportunities are for accredited investors only under Regulation D. Content is informational only — not a solicitation or offer to sell securities. Basin Ventures does not provide tax, legal, or financial advice. Past performance is not indicative of future results. Contact info@basinventures.com for permissions or commercial use.
